_meta.source: Pathfinder 2e Remaster + Inner Sea World Guide. Anchored to a known table date so weekday math always matches the GM's actual calendar.
_meta.rules: PF2e: leap year every 4 years (extra day at end of Calistril). 12 months, 7-day week, 364-365 days/year.

anchor._comment: Known fact: this date IS this weekday. The engine derives all other weekdays from this. Change only if your GM's calendar drifts from the official one.

months.name: Abadius
months.blurb: First month of the year; named for the god of cities, law, merchants, and wealth.

months.name: Calistril
months.blurb: Late winter; named for the goddess of revenge, lust, and trickery. Leap day appended every 4 years.

months.name: Pharast
months.blurb: Early spring; named for the goddess of birth, death, and prophecy.

months.name: Gozran
months.blurb: A stormy month; named for the dual god of nature and weather.

months.name: Desnus
months.blurb: A mild spring month; named for the goddess of dreams, stars, and travelers.

months.name: Sarenith
months.blurb: Sun-blessed early summer; named for the goddess of the sun, healing, and redemption.

months.name: Erastus
months.blurb: High summer; named for the god of family, farming, hunting, and trade.

months.name: Arodus
months.blurb: Late summer; named for the dead god of humanity. Still observed despite his death in 4606 AR.

months.name: Rova
months.blurb: Beginning of autumn; named for the imprisoned god of destruction. Dwarves call this month Torawsh after Torag instead.

months.name: Lamashan
months.blurb: Mid-autumn; named for the demon-mother goddess of monsters and nightmares.

months.name: Neth
months.blurb: Late autumn; named for the two-faced god of magic.

months.name: Kuthona
months.blurb: Deep winter; named for the god of darkness, envy, and pain. Contains the year's shortest day.

weekdays.name: Moonday
weekdays.blurb: Generally a work day; some religions hold ceremonies at night.

weekdays.name: Oathday
weekdays.blurb: A work day on which it is often considered advantageous to sign contracts and oaths.

weekdays.name: Fireday
weekdays.blurb: A work day, most often the market day.

weekdays.name: Sunday
weekdays.blurb: A day of rest and religious observance.

seasons._comment: Season boundaries map to calendar months. Used by the weather system to pick the right probability tables. Roughly aligned to real-world astronomical seasons.

holidays.name: Foundation Day
holidays.blurb: Anniversary of the founding of Absalom by Aroden in 1 AR. Widely celebrated across the Inner Sea.

holidays.name: Taxfest
holidays.blurb: An Abadaran holiday celebrating the orderly collection of revenue. Markets host special sales.

holidays.name: Currentseve
holidays.blurb: Sailors observe Gozreh's vigil; offerings are cast into rivers and seas.

holidays.name: Merrymead
holidays.blurb: A day of feasting and drinking honey-mead, popular among halflings and gnomes.

holidays.name: Day of Bones
holidays.blurb: Pharasmin holiday honoring the dead; mourners visit graves and leave offerings.

holidays.name: Spring Equinox
holidays.blurb: First day of spring. Druidic and Desnan ceremonies welcome the lengthening days.

holidays.name: Firstbloom
holidays.blurb: Gozren holiday celebrating the first blossoms of spring.

holidays.name: Evening of the Adored
holidays.blurb: Calistrian holiday of indulgence, romance, and revelry.

holidays.name: Ritual of Stardust
holidays.blurb: Desnan holiday; a night of songs, stargazing, and travel.

holidays.name: Ascendance Day
holidays.blurb: Anniversary of Aroden's ascent to godhood (long ago). Now also the start of the old summer festivals.

holidays.name: Sunwrought Festival
holidays.blurb: Sarenrae's holiday near the summer solstice. Communal sunrise prayers and forgiveness ceremonies.

holidays.name: Summer Solstice
holidays.blurb: Longest day of the year. Sun-themed ceremonies across many faiths.

holidays.name: Founding Day
holidays.blurb: Anniversary of various national foundings. Date varies by region; widely observed in Andoran.

holidays.name: Archerfeast
holidays.blurb: Erastilian holiday of hunting, archery contests, and family feasts.

holidays.name: Armasse
holidays.blurb: Iomedaean (and once Arodenite) festival of martial training. Knights swear new oaths.

holidays.name: First Brewing
holidays.blurb: Dwarven and Kellid celebration of the autumn brewing season.

holidays.name: Ruby Prince's Day
holidays.blurb: Osirian holiday celebrating the Ruby Prince's birthday.

holidays.name: Autumn Equinox
holidays.blurb: First day of autumn. Pharasmin and Desnan rites mark the turning of the year.

holidays.name: Harvest Feast
holidays.blurb: End of the harvest, with communal feasts. Especially important in rural Andoran and Taldor.

holidays.name: Night of the Pale
holidays.blurb: A vigil against undead and restless spirits. Doors are warded; candles burn until dawn.

holidays.name: Allcoming
holidays.blurb: Pathfinder Society holiday honoring all explorers and Pathfinders.

holidays.name: Trial of Ages
holidays.blurb: Pharasmin festival of remembrance for souls long-judged.

holidays.name: Ascendance Eve
holidays.blurb: Vigil before the Iomedaean ascendance celebrations.

holidays.name: Winter Solstice
holidays.blurb: Shortest day of the year. Crystalhue celebrations: candles, lanterns, and family.

holidays.name: Crystalhue
holidays.blurb: Solstice festival of light. Communities decorate with crystal and candles to ward off the long night.

holidays.name: Ascension Day
holidays.blurb: Iomedae's ascension to godhood (4607 AR). Knights and crusaders swear new oaths.

holidays.name: New Year's Eve
holidays.blurb: End of the year. Bonfires, toasts, and farewells to the past year.

moon._comment: Lunar cycle approximated as 29 days (Somal, Golarion's largest moon). Phase computed from days-since-anchor mod 29.
moon._anchorPhaseComment: On the anchor date (26 Gozran 4712 AR), assume Somal is approximately full (phase 14 of 29). This is a campaign convention; adjust if your GM has set a specific phase.
